summary: Julia built a web application that visualizes Git workflows based on the sequence of commands a user has executed. The tool generates visual diagrams showing how different Git commands relate to each other. She demonstrates it with Selena Deckelmann's workflow, which clearly illustrates when to use rebase and stash in a typical workflow.
key_points: Built a webapp that turns Git command histories into visual diagrams
Visualizations can reveal workflow patterns like when to use rebase vs stash
The tool makes implicit Git knowledge explicit through pictures
money_quotes: You tell it the sequence of git commands you've ever executed. It gives you back pretty pictures.
I really like how this one makes it clear when you'd want to use rebase (in between commit and push), or stash (before checkout and pull). It is a thing of beauty.
key_insight: Visualizing the sequence of Git commands you actually use can reveal workflow patterns and clarify when different commands are most useful.
